{"type":"system","subtype":"task_notification","task_id":"a72a243d78b6626fe","tool_use_id":"toolu_01GYEEvaqQn5eSjKeTTMbG4A","status":"completed","output_file":"","summary":"Code review of full codebase","usage":{"total_tokens":80086,"tool_uses":62,"duration_ms":144552},"uuid":"5e1769f2-b463-46ea-b0a9-63d01c9f6e22","session_id":"af8e20d7-729c-4922-9889-91614f9e372a"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GYEEvaqQn5eSjKeTTMbG4A","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I now have a complete picture of the codebase. Here is the full review.\n\n---\n\n## Bookmate Code Review — Round 8\n\nReviewing the full application: `app/lib/`, `app/app/api/`, and `app/components/`. All findings are high-confidence (80+).\n\n---\n\n## Critical Issues\n\n### 1. Timing-Safe Comparison Not Used for Webhook Secrets — Timing Oracle on Secret Tokens\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`, lines 31–33\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/discord/webhook/route.ts`, lines 28–30\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/telegram/setup/route.ts`, line 22\n**Confidence: 85**\n\nAll webhook and setup secrets are compared with JavaScript's `!==` operator, which performs a short-circuit string comparison. This leaks information about the secret via a timing side-channel — an attacker who can measure response latency can progressively guess the correct secret character by character.\n\n```ts\n// Vulnerable: short-circuit comparison\nif (headerSecret !== webhookSecret) { ... }\nif (body.secret !== setupSecret) { ... }\n```\n\nThe fix is `crypto.timingSafeEqual` on `Buffer.from(...)` representations of both strings, with a length pre-check that uses the constant-time path for mismatched lengths:\n\n```ts\nimport { timingSafeEqual } from \"crypto\";\n\nfunction safeCompare(a: string, b: string): boolean {\n  const bufA = Buffer.from(a);\n  const bufB = Buffer.from(b);\n  if (bufA.length !== bufB.length) return false;\n  return timingSafeEqual(bufA, bufB);\n}\n```\n\nThis is a lower-severity concern in practice because the server is behind a single machine with low-precision timing, but it is a genuine vulnerability class that is cheap to fix and directly relevant to secret-guarding endpoints.\n\n---\n\n## High Issues\n\n### 2. `maxGroupSize` Validated as Integer but Accepted as Any JSON Type — `parseInt` on Objects/Arrays Produces `NaN` Bypass\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 180–196\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/create/route.ts`, lines 75–81\n**Confidence: 85**\n\n`maxGroupSize` is parsed via `parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10)` without first verifying it is a string or number. If the client sends `maxGroupSize: [2]` (an array), `parseInt([2], 10)` returns `2` (JavaScript coerces the array to the string `\"2\"` before parsing), so the check passes. However if sent `maxGroupSize: {}` it becomes `NaN` and is correctly rejected. The more practical concern is that non-string, non-number values will silently coerce rather than be rejected, which is unexpected and fragile behavior.\n\nCompare to `applicationId` and `ratedUserId`, which are correctly validated with `typeof ... !== \"number\"` checks first. `maxGroupSize` should receive the same treatment. The same issue exists in `create/route.ts`.\n\n```ts\n// Missing type check — add this before parseInt:\nif (typeof maxGroupSize !== \"number\" && typeof maxGroupSize !== \"string\") {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid group size\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\n```\n\n### 3. `readingPace`, `startDate`, `meetingFormat` Not Type-Checked Before String Operations in PATCH\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 162–177\n**Confidence: 82**\n\nThe PATCH handler calls `.length`, `.trim()`, and `.test()` on `readingPace`, `startDate`, and `meetingFormat` from `req.json()` without confirming they are strings. If a client sends `readingPace: 123` (a number), `readingPace.length` is `undefined` in TypeScript runtime (numbers don't have `.length`) and the guard `!readingPace || readingPace.length > 200` silently passes (since `undefined > 200` is `false`), writing the number directly into the database. The DB column is `TEXT`, so SQLite will store it as a string, but it bypasses all validation intent.\n\nThe `language` field (line 199) correctly checks `typeof language !== \"string\"`. The other fields are missing the equivalent guard.\n\n```ts\nif (readingPace !== undefined && (typeof readingPace !== \"string\" || !readingPace || readingPace.length > 200)) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"...\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\n```\n\nSimilarly for `bookTitle`, `bookAuthor`, `readingPace`, and `meetingFormat` in `create/route.ts` — they all call `.length`, `.trim()`, `.includes()` without type guarding.\n\n### 4. Stale `listing.is_full` Check in `join/route.ts` Before the Transaction\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, lines 41–43\n**Confidence: 82**\n\nThe `listing` object is fetched with `SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?` at the top of the handler (outside the transaction), and then `listing.is_full` is checked at line 41 as a pre-flight gate. The transaction at line 71 re-checks with a fresh `COUNT(*)` and uses `count >= listing.max_group_size` — this is correct and atomic.\n\nHowever, the pre-transaction check on `listing.is_full` at line 41 uses a value that could be stale relative to when the transaction actually runs. More critically, `listing.max_group_size` used inside the transaction at line 76 also comes from the stale read. If the listing's `max_group_size` was changed by a concurrent PATCH between the outer `SELECT *` and the transaction execution, the transaction will enforce the old size limit, potentially admitting one extra member or blocking one valid join.\n\nThe solution is to re-read `max_group_size` inside the transaction (as `apply/route.ts` already does by re-reading `is_full` inside its transaction at line 59).\n\n### 5. Author Notification Sent Even When Join Is Author Re-Joining After Approval-Mode Toggle\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts`, lines 111–117\n**Confidence: 80**\n\nWhen an application is approved, the code calls `notifyListingAuthor(listingId, applicant.display_name)` at line 117, which creates a \"new_member\" notification for the listing author. This means the listing author receives a \"Someone joined your reading group\" notification about a member they themselves just approved — the author is the one performing the action, so this notification is noise at best, and a confusing duplicate at worst (the author also gets an `application_received` notification earlier in the flow).\n\nThe `notifyListingAuthor` call is appropriate for the direct-join flow (where the author learns passively), but for the approval flow the author is the actor and already knows the outcome. This is a logic error that will generate spurious notifications for every approval.\n\n---\n\n## Important Issues\n\n### 6. `listing.is_full` Is Not Re-checked Inside the `apply` Transaction When `requiresApproval` Is True, But `is_full` Guard Before The Transaction Uses Stale Data\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts`, lines 43–44 and 63–65\n**Confidence: 81**\n\nThe apply route has a pre-transaction `if (listing.is_full)` check at line 43 using the outer stale read, and then re-checks `is_full` from a fresh DB read inside the transaction at line 59. This is good defensive design, but there is a gap: the outer stale `listing` object's `is_full` can be `0` while the inner transaction reads it as `1` (group was just filled by a concurrent join/approval). The outer guard would pass, causing an unnecessary transaction to execute, but the inner guard correctly stops it. No correctness bug — but it is worth noting the pre-flight check at line 43 may mislead future readers into thinking it provides security rather than just being a quick client-visible short-circuit.\n\nThis is minor — the inner transaction guard is correct. Noted for completeness at confidence 81 (real but not harmful).\n\n### 7. Email HTML Content Not Escaped — Potential Email Header/Body Injection via Notification Messages\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`, lines 69–75 and `app/lib/email.ts`, line 41\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n`createNotification` passes raw user-controlled content in the `message` parameter directly to `sendEmail` as both the plain-text `text` and the fallback `html` body (see `email.ts` line 41: `html: html || text`). Since no separate `html` argument is passed, the plain text message becomes the HTML body verbatim.\n\nNotification messages include user-supplied data: book titles (from Open Library, validated to max 300 chars), display names (from user-controlled input, validated to max 100 chars), and applicant names. A display name of `<script>alert(1)</script>` stored in the DB would be injected directly into the HTML email body.\n\nThe fix is either: (a) pass a separately HTML-escaped `html` argument to `sendEmail`, or (b) HTML-encode the message before embedding it in the email body.\n\n---\n\n## Summary Table\n\n| # | Severity | File | Lines | Issue |\n|---|----------|------|-------|-------|\n| 1 | Critical | `api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`, `api/discord/webhook/route.ts`, `api/telegram/setup/route.ts` | 31–33, 28–30, 22 | Non-timing-safe secret comparison (timing oracle) |\n| 2 | High | `api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, `api/listings/create/route.ts` | 180–196, 75–81 | `maxGroupSize` accepts non-string/number types through `parseInt` coercion |\n| 3 | High | `api/listings/[id]/route.ts` | 162–177 | `readingPace`, `startDate`, `meetingFormat` not type-checked before string operations |\n| 4 | High | `api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts` | 41–43, 76 | Stale `listing.max_group_size` used inside transaction |\n| 5 | High | `api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts` | 111–117 | Author notified of their own approval action (\"new_member\") |\n| 6 | Important | `api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts` | 43–44 | Stale pre-transaction `is_full` guard (benign, inner guard is correct) |\n| 7 | Important | `lib/notifications.ts`, `lib/email.ts` | 69–75, 41 | User-controlled notification messages injected into HTML email body unescaped |"},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a72a243d78b6626fe (use SendMessage with to: 'a72a243d78b6626fe' to continue this agent)\n<usage>total_tokens: 80954\ntool_uses: 62\nduration_ms: 144555</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"af8e20d7-729c-4922-9889-91614f9e372a","uuid":"b5639e73-3fcc-4a6b-adda-6b9970202f5e","timestamp":"2026-03-24T05:03:55.982Z","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Review the Bookmate Next.js application in /root/Projects/bookmate/app for bugs, security issues, code quality problems, and logic errors. The app uses Next.js 15 App Router, TypeScript, SQLite (better-sqlite3), iron-session, and Tailwind CSS v4.\n\nFocus on:\n1. API routes in app/app/api/ — look for security issues, missing validation, race conditions\n2. Database queries in app/lib/db.ts — look for SQL injection, missing indexes, transaction safety\n3. Session/auth handling in app/lib/session.ts and middleware\n4. React components — look for rendering bugs, missing error handling\n5. Any patterns that could lead to data loss or corruption\n\nThe codebase has already been through 7 rounds of code review, so focus on subtle issues that might have been missed. Only report HIGH confidence issues — no style nits or minor suggestions.\n\nStart by reading the key files:\n- app/lib/db.ts\n- app/lib/session.ts  \n- app/lib/notifications.ts\n- app/app/api/ routes\n- app/middleware.ts\n- app/components/\n\nReport each issue with: file path, line number range, severity (critical/high/medium), and a description of the problem.","agentId":"a72a243d78b6626fe","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I now have a complete picture of the codebase. Here is the full review.\n\n---\n\n## Bookmate Code Review — Round 8\n\nReviewing the full application: `app/lib/`, `app/app/api/`, and `app/components/`. All findings are high-confidence (80+).\n\n---\n\n## Critical Issues\n\n### 1. Timing-Safe Comparison Not Used for Webhook Secrets — Timing Oracle on Secret Tokens\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`, lines 31–33\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/discord/webhook/route.ts`, lines 28–30\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/telegram/setup/route.ts`, line 22\n**Confidence: 85**\n\nAll webhook and setup secrets are compared with JavaScript's `!==` operator, which performs a short-circuit string comparison. This leaks information about the secret via a timing side-channel — an attacker who can measure response latency can progressively guess the correct secret character by character.\n\n```ts\n// Vulnerable: short-circuit comparison\nif (headerSecret !== webhookSecret) { ... }\nif (body.secret !== setupSecret) { ... }\n```\n\nThe fix is `crypto.timingSafeEqual` on `Buffer.from(...)` representations of both strings, with a length pre-check that uses the constant-time path for mismatched lengths:\n\n```ts\nimport { timingSafeEqual } from \"crypto\";\n\nfunction safeCompare(a: string, b: string): boolean {\n  const bufA = Buffer.from(a);\n  const bufB = Buffer.from(b);\n  if (bufA.length !== bufB.length) return false;\n  return timingSafeEqual(bufA, bufB);\n}\n```\n\nThis is a lower-severity concern in practice because the server is behind a single machine with low-precision timing, but it is a genuine vulnerability class that is cheap to fix and directly relevant to secret-guarding endpoints.\n\n---\n\n## High Issues\n\n### 2. `maxGroupSize` Validated as Integer but Accepted as Any JSON Type — `parseInt` on Objects/Arrays Produces `NaN` Bypass\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 180–196\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/create/route.ts`, lines 75–81\n**Confidence: 85**\n\n`maxGroupSize` is parsed via `parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10)` without first verifying it is a string or number. If the client sends `maxGroupSize: [2]` (an array), `parseInt([2], 10)` returns `2` (JavaScript coerces the array to the string `\"2\"` before parsing), so the check passes. However if sent `maxGroupSize: {}` it becomes `NaN` and is correctly rejected. The more practical concern is that non-string, non-number values will silently coerce rather than be rejected, which is unexpected and fragile behavior.\n\nCompare to `applicationId` and `ratedUserId`, which are correctly validated with `typeof ... !== \"number\"` checks first. `maxGroupSize` should receive the same treatment. The same issue exists in `create/route.ts`.\n\n```ts\n// Missing type check — add this before parseInt:\nif (typeof maxGroupSize !== \"number\" && typeof maxGroupSize !== \"string\") {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid group size\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\n```\n\n### 3. `readingPace`, `startDate`, `meetingFormat` Not Type-Checked Before String Operations in PATCH\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 162–177\n**Confidence: 82**\n\nThe PATCH handler calls `.length`, `.trim()`, and `.test()` on `readingPace`, `startDate`, and `meetingFormat` from `req.json()` without confirming they are strings. If a client sends `readingPace: 123` (a number), `readingPace.length` is `undefined` in TypeScript runtime (numbers don't have `.length`) and the guard `!readingPace || readingPace.length > 200` silently passes (since `undefined > 200` is `false`), writing the number directly into the database. The DB column is `TEXT`, so SQLite will store it as a string, but it bypasses all validation intent.\n\nThe `language` field (line 199) correctly checks `typeof language !== \"string\"`. The other fields are missing the equivalent guard.\n\n```ts\nif (readingPace !== undefined && (typeof readingPace !== \"string\" || !readingPace || readingPace.length > 200)) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"...\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\n```\n\nSimilarly for `bookTitle`, `bookAuthor`, `readingPace`, and `meetingFormat` in `create/route.ts` — they all call `.length`, `.trim()`, `.includes()` without type guarding.\n\n### 4. Stale `listing.is_full` Check in `join/route.ts` Before the Transaction\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, lines 41–43\n**Confidence: 82**\n\nThe `listing` object is fetched with `SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?` at the top of the handler (outside the transaction), and then `listing.is_full` is checked at line 41 as a pre-flight gate. The transaction at line 71 re-checks with a fresh `COUNT(*)` and uses `count >= listing.max_group_size` — this is correct and atomic.\n\nHowever, the pre-transaction check on `listing.is_full` at line 41 uses a value that could be stale relative to when the transaction actually runs. More critically, `listing.max_group_size` used inside the transaction at line 76 also comes from the stale read. If the listing's `max_group_size` was changed by a concurrent PATCH between the outer `SELECT *` and the transaction execution, the transaction will enforce the old size limit, potentially admitting one extra member or blocking one valid join.\n\nThe solution is to re-read `max_group_size` inside the transaction (as `apply/route.ts` already does by re-reading `is_full` inside its transaction at line 59).\n\n### 5. Author Notification Sent Even When Join Is Author Re-Joining After Approval-Mode Toggle\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts`, lines 111–117\n**Confidence: 80**\n\nWhen an application is approved, the code calls `notifyListingAuthor(listingId, applicant.display_name)` at line 117, which creates a \"new_member\" notification for the listing author. This means the listing author receives a \"Someone joined your reading group\" notification about a member they themselves just approved — the author is the one performing the action, so this notification is noise at best, and a confusing duplicate at worst (the author also gets an `application_received` notification earlier in the flow).\n\nThe `notifyListingAuthor` call is appropriate for the direct-join flow (where the author learns passively), but for the approval flow the author is the actor and already knows the outcome. This is a logic error that will generate spurious notifications for every approval.\n\n---\n\n## Important Issues\n\n### 6. `listing.is_full` Is Not Re-checked Inside the `apply` Transaction When `requiresApproval` Is True, But `is_full` Guard Before The Transaction Uses Stale Data\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts`, lines 43–44 and 63–65\n**Confidence: 81**\n\nThe apply route has a pre-transaction `if (listing.is_full)` check at line 43 using the outer stale read, and then re-checks `is_full` from a fresh DB read inside the transaction at line 59. This is good defensive design, but there is a gap: the outer stale `listing` object's `is_full` can be `0` while the inner transaction reads it as `1` (group was just filled by a concurrent join/approval). The outer guard would pass, causing an unnecessary transaction to execute, but the inner guard correctly stops it. No correctness bug — but it is worth noting the pre-flight check at line 43 may mislead future readers into thinking it provides security rather than just being a quick client-visible short-circuit.\n\nThis is minor — the inner transaction guard is correct. Noted for completeness at confidence 81 (real but not harmful).\n\n### 7. Email HTML Content Not Escaped — Potential Email Header/Body Injection via Notification Messages\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`, lines 69–75 and `app/lib/email.ts`, line 41\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n`createNotification` passes raw user-controlled content in the `message` parameter directly to `sendEmail` as both the plain-text `text` and the fallback `html` body (see `email.ts` line 41: `html: html || text`). Since no separate `html` argument is passed, the plain text message becomes the HTML body verbatim.\n\nNotification messages include user-supplied data: book titles (from Open Library, validated to max 300 chars), display names (from user-controlled input, validated to max 100 chars), and applicant names. A display name of `<script>alert(1)</script>` stored in the DB would be injected directly into the HTML email body.\n\nThe fix is either: (a) pass a separately HTML-escaped `html` argument to `sendEmail`, or (b) HTML-encode the message before embedding it in the email body.\n\n---\n\n## Summary Table\n\n| # | Severity | File | Lines | Issue |\n|---|----------|------|-------|-------|\n| 1 | Critical | `api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`, `api/discord/webhook/route.ts`, `api/telegram/setup/route.ts` | 31–33, 28–30, 22 | Non-timing-safe secret comparison (timing oracle) |\n| 2 | High | `api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, `api/listings/create/route.ts` | 180–196, 75–81 | `maxGroupSize` accepts non-string/number types through `parseInt` coercion |\n| 3 | High | `api/listings/[id]/route.ts` | 162–177 | `readingPace`, `startDate`, `meetingFormat` not type-checked before string operations |\n| 4 | High | `api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts` | 41–43, 76 | Stale `listing.max_group_size` used inside transaction |\n| 5 | High | `api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts` | 111–117 | Author notified of their own approval action (\"new_member\") |\n| 6 | Important | `api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts` | 43–44 | Stale pre-transaction `is_full` guard (benign, inner guard is correct) |\n| 7 | Important | `lib/notifications.ts`, `lib/email.ts` | 69–75, 41 | User-controlled notification messages injected into HTML email body unescaped |"}],"totalDurationMs":144555,"totalTokens":80954,"totalToolUseCount":62,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":474,"cache_read_input_tokens":77712,"output_tokens":2767,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":474},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_011HMwrtP3ngVvb2WGEtMtFx","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import {\n     4→  createChatInviteLink,\n     5→  exportChatInviteLink,\n     6→  sendMessage,\n     7→  parseListingIdFromPayload,\n     8→  escapeHtml,\n     9→} from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n    10→import { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n    11→\n    12→/**\n    13→ * Telegram webhook endpoint.\n    14→ *\n    15→ * Handles two types of updates:\n    16→ *\n    17→ * 1. `my_chat_member` — bot was added to a group\n    18→ *    When the author creates a group via the deep link, the bot is\n    19→ *    automatically added. We detect this, generate an invite link,\n    20→ *    and save it to the listing.\n    21→ *\n    22→ * 2. `message` with `/start listing_ID` — the bot receives a start\n    23→ *    command in a group with a payload referencing a listing.\n    24→ */\n    25→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n    26→  // Verify webhook secret — fail closed if not configured\n    27→  const webhookSecret = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET;\n    28→  if (!webhookSecret) {\n    29→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Webhook not configured\" }, { status: 500 });\n    30→  }\n    31→  const headerSecret = req.headers.get(\"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\");\n    32→  if (headerSecret !== webhookSecret) {\n    33→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n    34→  }\n    35→\n    36→  let update: Record<string, unknown>;\n    37→  try {\n    38→    update = await req.json();\n    39→  } catch {\n    40→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid JSON\" }, { status: 400 });\n    41→  }\n    42→\n    43→  // Handle bot being added to a group chat\n    44→  if (update.my_chat_member) {\n    45→    await handleChatMemberUpdate(\n    46→      update.my_chat_member as ChatMemberUpdate\n    47→    );\n    48→  }\n    49→\n    50→  // Handle /start command in groups (fallback mechanism)\n    51→  if (update.message) {\n    52→    await handleMessage(update.message as TelegramMessage);\n    53→  }\n    54→\n    55→  // Always return 200 to Telegram to avoid retries\n    56→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    57→}\n    58→\n    59→interface ChatMemberUpdate {\n    60→  chat: {\n    61→    id: number;\n    62→    title?: string;\n    63→    type: string;\n    64→  };\n    65→  from: {\n    66→    id: number;\n    67→    first_name: string;\n    68→  };\n    69→  new_chat_member: {\n    70→    user: {\n    71→      id: number;\n    72→      is_bot: boolean;\n    73→    };\n    74→    status: string;\n    75→  };\n    76→}\n    77→\n    78→interface TelegramMessage {\n    79→  chat: {\n    80→    id: number;\n    81→    type: string;\n    82→    title?: string;\n    83→  };\n    84→  text?: string;\n    85→  from?: {\n    86→    id: number;\n    87→  };\n    88→}\n    89→\n    90→/**\n    91→ * When the bot is added to a group, check if it's related to a listing.\n    92→ *\n    93→ * The deep link format is: t.me/BOT?startgroup=listing_ID\n    94→ * When a user creates a group through this link, Telegram:\n    95→ * 1. Creates the group\n    96→ * 2. Adds the bot as a member\n    97→ * 3. Sends a `my_chat_member` update\n    98→ *\n    99→ * We use the group title or a pending mapping to associate the chat with a listing.\n   100→ */\n   101→async function handleChatMemberUpdate(update: ChatMemberUpdate) {\n   102→  const { chat, new_chat_member } = update;\n   103→\n   104→  // Only process when bot is added (member or administrator)\n   105→  if (\n   106→    !new_chat_member.user.is_bot ||\n   107→    ![\"member\", \"administrator\"].includes(new_chat_member.status)\n   108→  ) {\n   109→    return;\n   110→  }\n   111→\n   112→  // Only process group chats\n   113→  if (![\"group\", \"supergroup\"].includes(chat.type)) {\n   114→    return;\n   115→  }\n   116→\n   117→  // The bot was added to a group. Since we can't determine the listing from\n   118→  // the my_chat_member event alone, send a welcome message and wait for the\n   119→  // /start payload or /link command to establish the association.\n   120→  await sendMessage(\n   121→    chat.id,\n   122→    \"Hello! I'm the Bookmate bot. I'll help set up this reading group.\\n\\n\" +\n   123→      'If this group was created for a Bookmate listing, I\\'ll automatically generate an invite link. ' +\n   124→      \"The organizer can also type <code>/link</code> to manually trigger the setup.\"\n   125→  );\n   126→\n   127→  // Store the chat for potential later linking\n   128→  try {\n   129→    db.prepare(\n   130→      \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO telegram_chats (chat_id, chat_title, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, datetime('now'))\"\n   131→    ).run(chat.id, chat.title || \"\");\n   132→  } catch {\n   133→    // Table might not exist yet on first run — handled gracefully\n   134→  }\n   135→}\n   136→\n   137→/**\n   138→ * Handle messages in group chats.\n   139→ * Supports /start with payload and /link command.\n   140→ */\n   141→async function handleMessage(message: TelegramMessage) {\n   142→  if (!message.text || ![\"group\", \"supergroup\"].includes(message.chat.type)) {\n   143→    return;\n   144→  }\n   145→\n   146→  const text = message.text.trim();\n   147→\n   148→  // Handle /start with listing payload (sent when group is created via deep link)\n   149→  if (text.startsWith(\"/start \")) {\n   150→    const payload = text.replace(\"/start \", \"\").trim();\n   151→    const listingId = parseListingIdFromPayload(payload);\n   152→    if (listingId) {\n   153→      // Verify the sender is the listing author to prevent unauthorized linking\n   154→      const telegramUserId = message.from?.id;\n   155→      if (!telegramUserId) {\n   156→        await sendMessage(message.chat.id, \"Could not identify the sender.\");\n   157→        return;\n   158→      }\n   159→\n   160→      const linkedUser = db\n   161→        .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM telegram_user_links WHERE telegram_user_id = ?\")\n   162→        .get(telegramUserId) as { user_id: number } | undefined;\n   163→\n   164→      if (linkedUser) {\n   165→        const listing = db\n   166→          .prepare(\"SELECT author_id FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   167→          .get(listingId) as { author_id: number } | undefined;\n   168→\n   169→        if (listing && listing.author_id === linkedUser.user_id) {\n   170→          await linkTelegramToListing(listingId, message.chat.id);\n   171→          return;\n   172→        }\n   173→      }\n   174→\n   175→      // If no linked user or not the author, store for later linking via /link command\n   176→      await sendMessage(\n   177→        message.chat.id,\n   178→        \"To link this group to a Bookmate listing, the listing author should use the <code>/link</code> command.\"\n   179→      );\n   180→      return;\n   181→    }\n   182→  }\n   183→\n   184→  // Handle /link command — allow manual linking\n   185→  // Security: only show listings authored by a Bookmate user linked to this Telegram user\n   186→  if (text === \"/link\") {\n   187→    const telegramUserId = message.from?.id;\n   188→    if (!telegramUserId) {\n   189→      await sendMessage(message.chat.id, \"Could not identify the sender.\");\n   190→      return;\n   191→    }\n   192→\n   193→    // Look up the Bookmate user linked to this Telegram user\n   194→    const linkedUser = db\n   195→      .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM telegram_user_links WHERE telegram_user_id = ?\")\n   196→      .get(telegramUserId) as { user_id: number } | undefined;\n   197→\n   198→    if (!linkedUser) {\n   199→      await sendMessage(\n   200→        message.chat.id,\n   201→        \"Your Telegram account is not linked to a Bookmate account. \" +\n   202→          \"Please use the Telegram setup link from your Bookmate listing page to link your account first.\"\n   203→      );\n   204→      return;\n   205→    }\n   206→\n   207→    // Only show unlinked listings authored by this verified user\n   208→    const unlinkedListings = db\n   209→      .prepare(\n   210→        `SELECT id, book_title FROM listings\n   211→         WHERE is_full = 1 AND (telegram_link = '' OR telegram_link IS NULL)\n   212→         AND telegram_chat_id IS NULL\n   213→         AND author_id = ?\n   214→         ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 5`\n   215→      )\n   216→      .all(linkedUser.user_id) as { id: number; book_title: string }[];\n   217→\n   218→    if (unlinkedListings.length === 0) {\n   219→      await sendMessage(\n   220→        message.chat.id,\n   221→        \"No unlinked reading groups found for your account. The group may already be set up!\"\n   222→      );\n   223→      return;\n   224→    }\n   225→\n   226→    if (unlinkedListings.length === 1) {\n   227→      await linkTelegramToListing(unlinkedListings[0].id, message.chat.id);\n   228→      return;\n   229→    }\n   230→\n   231→    const listText = unlinkedListings\n   232→      .map((l) => `  /link_${l.id} — \"${escapeHtml(l.book_title)}\"`)\n   233→      .join(\"\\n\");\n   234→    await sendMessage(\n   235→      message.chat.id,\n   236→      `Multiple reading groups need a Telegram link. Pick one:\\n\\n${listText}`\n   237→    );\n   238→    return;\n   239→  }\n   240→\n   241→  // Handle /link_ID command — require verified ownership\n   242→  const linkMatch = text.match(/^\\/link_(\\d+)$/);\n   243→  if (linkMatch) {\n   244→    const telegramUserId = message.from?.id;\n   245→    if (!telegramUserId) {\n   246→      await sendMessage(message.chat.id, \"Could not identify the sender.\");\n   247→      return;\n   248→    }\n   249→\n   250→    const linkedUser = db\n   251→      .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM telegram_user_links WHERE telegram_user_id = ?\")\n   252→      .get(telegramUserId) as { user_id: number } | undefined;\n   253→\n   254→    if (!linkedUser) {\n   255→      await sendMessage(\n   256→        message.chat.id,\n   257→        \"Your Telegram account is not linked to a Bookmate account.\"\n   258→      );\n   259→      return;\n   260→    }\n   261→\n   262→    const listingId = parseInt(linkMatch[1], 10);\n   263→\n   264→    // Verify this user is the listing author and the listing is full\n   265→    const listing = db\n   266→      .prepare(\"SELECT author_id, is_full FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   267→      .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; is_full: number } | undefined;\n   268→\n   269→    if (!listing || listing.author_id !== linkedUser.user_id) {\n   270→      await sendMessage(\n   271→        message.chat.id,\n   272→        \"You can only link Telegram groups to your own Bookmate listings.\"\n   273→      );\n   274→      return;\n   275→    }\n   276→\n   277→    if (!listing.is_full) {\n   278→      await sendMessage(\n   279→        message.chat.id,\n   280→        \"This listing's group is not full yet. You can link a Telegram group once the group reaches its maximum size.\"\n   281→      );\n   282→      return;\n   283→    }\n   284→\n   285→    await linkTelegramToListing(listingId, message.chat.id);\n   286→  }\n   287→}\n   288→\n   289→/**\n   290→ * Link a Telegram chat to a listing:\n   291→ * 1. Generate an invite link\n   292→ * 2. Save it to the listing\n   293→ * 3. Notify all members\n   294→ * 4. Send a confirmation message to the Telegram group\n   295→ */\n   296→async function linkTelegramToListing(listingId: number, chatId: number) {\n   297→  const listing = db\n   298→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   299→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n   300→\n   301→  if (!listing) {\n   302→    await sendMessage(chatId, \"Could not find that reading group on Bookmate.\");\n   303→    return;\n   304→  }\n   305→\n   306→  if (listing.telegram_link) {\n   307→    await sendMessage(chatId, \"This reading group already has a Telegram link set up!\");\n   308→    return;\n   309→  }\n   310→\n   311→  // Try to create an invite link\n   312→  let inviteLink = await createChatInviteLink(chatId);\n   313→  if (!inviteLink) {\n   314→    // Fallback: export the default invite link\n   315→    inviteLink = await exportChatInviteLink(chatId);\n   316→  }\n   317→\n   318→  if (!inviteLink) {\n   319→    await sendMessage(\n   320→      chatId,\n   321→      \"I couldn't generate an invite link. Please make sure I have admin permissions in this group, \" +\n   322→        \"then try again with /link\"\n   323→    );\n   324→    return;\n   325→  }\n   326→\n   327→  // Save to database\n   328→  db.prepare(\n   329→    \"UPDATE listings SET telegram_link = ?, telegram_chat_id = ? WHERE id = ?\"\n   330→  ).run(inviteLink, chatId, listingId);\n   331→\n   332→  // Notify all members on Bookmate\n   333→  const members = db\n   334→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   335→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   336→\n   337→  const bookTitle = listing.book_title as string;\n   338→  for (const member of members) {\n   339→    createNotification(\n   340→      member.user_id,\n   341→      listingId,\n   342→      \"telegram_ready\",\n   343→      `The Telegram group for \"${bookTitle}\" is ready! Click to view the invite link.`\n   344→    );\n   345→  }\n   346→\n   347→  // Send confirmation to the Telegram group\n   348→  // Escape book title to prevent HTML injection via Telegram's HTML parser\n   349→  await sendMessage(\n   350→    chatId,\n   351→    `This group is now linked to the Bookmate reading group for \"<b>${escapeHtml(bookTitle)}</b>\".\\n\\n` +\n   352→      `Invite link: ${inviteLink}\\n\\n` +\n   353→      \"All group members have been notified on Bookmate. Happy reading!\"\n   354→  );\n   355→}\n   356→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"af8e20d7-729c-4922-9889-91614f9e372a","uuid":"12d33359-f20b-485e-9b84-c7104b1b3ee7","timestamp":"2026-03-24T05:04:02.616Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport {\n  createChatInviteLink,\n  exportChatInviteLink,\n  sendMessage,\n  parseListingIdFromPayload,\n  escapeHtml,\n} from \"@/lib/telegram\";\nimport { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n\n/**\n * Telegram webhook endpoint.\n *\n * Handles two types of updates:\n *\n * 1. `my_chat_member` — bot was added to a group\n *    When the author creates a group via the deep link, the bot is\n *    automatically added. We detect this, generate an invite link,\n *    and save it to the listing.\n *\n * 2. `message` with `/start listing_ID` — the bot receives a start\n *    command in a group with a payload referencing a listing.\n */\nexport async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n  // Verify webhook secret — fail closed if not configured\n  const webhookSecret = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET;\n  if (!webhookSecret) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Webhook not configured\" }, { status: 500 });\n  }\n  const headerSecret = req.headers.get(\"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\");\n  if (headerSecret !== webhookSecret) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n  }\n\n  let update: Record<string, unknown>;\n  try {\n    update = await req.json();\n  } catch {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid JSON\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  // Handle bot being added to a group chat\n  if (update.my_chat_member) {\n    await handleChatMemberUpdate(\n      update.my_chat_member as ChatMemberUpdate\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Handle /start command in groups (fallback mechanism)\n  if (update.message) {\n    await handleMessage(update.message as TelegramMessage);\n  }\n\n  // Always return 200 to Telegram to avoid retries\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n}\n\ninterface ChatMemberUpdate {\n  chat: {\n    id: number;\n    title?: string;\n    type: string;\n  };\n  from: {\n    id: number;\n    first_name: string;\n  };\n  new_chat_member: {\n    user: {\n      id: number;\n      is_bot: boolean;\n    };\n    status: string;\n  };\n}\n\ninterface TelegramMessage {\n  chat: {\n    id: number;\n    type: string;\n    title?: string;\n  };\n  text?: string;\n  from?: {\n    id: number;\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * When the bot is added to a group, check if it's related to a listing.\n *\n * The deep link format is: t.me/BOT?startgroup=listing_ID\n * When a user creates a group through this link, Telegram:\n * 1. Creates the group\n * 2. Adds the bot as a member\n * 3. Sends a `my_chat_member` update\n *\n * We use the group title or a pending mapping to associate the chat with a listing.\n */\nasync function handleChatMemberUpdate(update: ChatMemberUpdate) {\n  const { chat, new_chat_member } = update;\n\n  // Only process when bot is added (member or administrator)\n  if (\n    !new_chat_member.user.is_bot ||\n    ![\"member\", \"administrator\"].includes(new_chat_member.status)\n  ) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Only process group chats\n  if (![\"group\", \"supergroup\"].includes(chat.type)) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // The bot was added to a group. Since we can't determine the listing from\n  // the my_chat_member event alone, send a welcome message and wait for the\n  // /start payload or /link command to establish the association.\n  await sendMessage(\n    chat.id,\n    \"Hello! I'm the Bookmate bot. I'll help set up this reading group.\\n\\n\" +\n      'If this group was created for a Bookmate listing, I\\'ll automatically generate an invite link. ' +\n      \"The organizer can also type <code>/link</code> to manually trigger the setup.\"\n  );\n\n  // Store the chat for potential later linking\n  try {\n    db.prepare(\n      \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO telegram_chats (chat_id, chat_title, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, datetime('now'))\"\n    ).run(chat.id, chat.title || \"\");\n  } catch {\n    // Table might not exist yet on first run — handled gracefully\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Handle messages in group chats.\n * Supports /start with payload and /link command.\n */\nasync function handleMessage(message: TelegramMessage) {\n  if (!message.text || ![\"group\", \"supergroup\"].includes(message.chat.type)) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const text = message.text.trim();\n\n  // Handle /start with listing payload (sent when group is created via deep link)\n  if (text.startsWith(\"/start \")) {\n    const payload = text.replace(\"/start \", \"\").trim();\n    const listingId = parseListingIdFromPayload(payload);\n    if (listingId) {\n      // Verify the sender is the listing author to prevent unauthorized linking\n      const telegramUserId = message.from?.id;\n      if (!telegramUserId) {\n        await sendMessage(message.chat.id, \"Could not identify the sender.\");\n        return;\n      }\n\n      const linkedUser = db\n        .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM telegram_user_links WHERE telegram_user_id = ?\")\n        .get(telegramUserId) as { user_id: number } | undefined;\n\n      if (linkedUser) {\n        const listing = db\n          .prepare(\"SELECT author_id FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n          .get(listingId) as { author_id: number } | undefined;\n\n        if (listing && listing.author_id === linkedUser.user_id) {\n          await linkTelegramToListing(listingId, message.chat.id);\n          return;\n        }\n      }\n\n      // If no linked user or not the author, store for later linking via /link command\n      await sendMessage(\n        message.chat.id,\n        \"To link this group to a Bookmate listing, the listing author should use the <code>/link</code> command.\"\n      );\n      return;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Handle /link command — allow manual linking\n  // Security: only show listings authored by a Bookmate user linked to this Telegram user\n  if (text === \"/link\") {\n    const telegramUserId = message.from?.id;\n    if (!telegramUserId) {\n      await sendMessage(message.chat.id, \"Could not identify the sender.\");\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // Look up the Bookmate user linked to this Telegram user\n    const linkedUser = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM telegram_user_links WHERE telegram_user_id = ?\")\n      .get(telegramUserId) as { user_id: number } | undefined;\n\n    if (!linkedUser) {\n      await sendMessage(\n        message.chat.id,\n        \"Your Telegram account is not linked to a Bookmate account. \" +\n          \"Please use the Telegram setup link from your Bookmate listing page to link your account first.\"\n      );\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // Only show unlinked listings authored by this verified user\n    const unlinkedListings = db\n      .prepare(\n        `SELECT id, book_title FROM listings\n         WHERE is_full = 1 AND (telegram_link = '' OR telegram_link IS NULL)\n         AND telegram_chat_id IS NULL\n         AND author_id = ?\n         ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 5`\n      )\n      .all(linkedUser.user_id) as { id: number; book_title: string }[];\n\n    if (unlinkedListings.length === 0) {\n      await sendMessage(\n        message.chat.id,\n        \"No unlinked reading groups found for your account. The group may already be set up!\"\n      );\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (unlinkedListings.length === 1) {\n      await linkTelegramToListing(unlinkedListings[0].id, message.chat.id);\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const listText = unlinkedListings\n      .map((l) => `  /link_${l.id} — \"${escapeHtml(l.book_title)}\"`)\n      .join(\"\\n\");\n    await sendMessage(\n      message.chat.id,\n      `Multiple reading groups need a Telegram link. Pick one:\\n\\n${listText}`\n    );\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Handle /link_ID command — require verified ownership\n  const linkMatch = text.match(/^\\/link_(\\d+)$/);\n  if (linkMatch) {\n    const telegramUserId = message.from?.id;\n    if (!telegramUserId) {\n      await sendMessage(message.chat.id, \"Could not identify the sender.\");\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const linkedUser = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM telegram_user_links WHERE telegram_user_id = ?\")\n      .get(telegramUserId) as { user_id: number } | undefined;\n\n    if (!linkedUser) {\n      await sendMessage(\n        message.chat.id,\n        \"Your Telegram account is not linked to a Bookmate account.\"\n      );\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const listingId = parseInt(linkMatch[1], 10);\n\n    // Verify this user is the listing author and the listing is full\n    const listing = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT author_id, is_full FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n      .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; is_full: number } | undefined;\n\n    if (!listing || listing.author_id !== linkedUser.user_id) {\n      await sendMessage(\n        message.chat.id,\n        \"You can only link Telegram groups to your own Bookmate listings.\"\n      );\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (!listing.is_full) {\n      await sendMessage(\n        message.chat.id,\n        \"This listing's group is not full yet. You can link a Telegram group once the group reaches its maximum size.\"\n      );\n      return;\n    }\n\n    await linkTelegramToListing(listingId, message.chat.id);\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Link a Telegram chat to a listing:\n * 1. Generate an invite link\n * 2. Save it to the listing\n * 3. Notify all members\n * 4. Send a confirmation message to the Telegram group\n */\nasync function linkTelegramToListing(listingId: number, chatId: number) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    await sendMessage(chatId, \"Could not find that reading group on Bookmate.\");\n    return;\n  }\n\n  if (listing.telegram_link) {\n    await sendMessage(chatId, \"This reading group already has a Telegram link set up!\");\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Try to create an invite link\n  let inviteLink = await createChatInviteLink(chatId);\n  if (!inviteLink) {\n    // Fallback: export the default invite link\n    inviteLink = await exportChatInviteLink(chatId);\n  }\n\n  if (!inviteLink) {\n    await sendMessage(\n      chatId,\n      \"I couldn't generate an invite link. Please make sure I have admin permissions in this group, \" +\n        \"then try again with /link\"\n    );\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Save to database\n  db.prepare(\n    \"UPDATE listings SET telegram_link = ?, telegram_chat_id = ? WHERE id = ?\"\n  ).run(inviteLink, chatId, listingId);\n\n  // Notify all members on Bookmate\n  const members = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n\n  const bookTitle = listing.book_title as string;\n  for (const member of members) {\n    createNotification(\n      member.user_id,\n      listingId,\n      \"telegram_ready\",\n      `The Telegram group for \"${bookTitle}\" is ready! Click to view the invite link.`\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Send confirmation to the Telegram group\n  // Escape book title to prevent HTML injection via Telegram's HTML parser\n  await sendMessage(\n    chatId,\n    `This group is now linked to the Bookmate reading group for \"<b>${escapeHtml(bookTitle)}</b>\".\\n\\n` +\n      `Invite link: ${inviteLink}\\n\\n` +\n      \"All group members have been notified on Bookmate. Happy reading!\"\n  );\n}\n","numLines":356,"startLine":1,"totalLines":356}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018BGa4YfU6QWwxY8cZ2cxbc","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import {\n     4→  createTextChannel,\n     5→  createChannelInvite,\n     6→  sendChannelMessage,\n     7→  isValidSnowflake,\n     8→} from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     9→import { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n    10→\n    11→/**\n    12→ * Discord webhook endpoint for bot events.\n    13→ *\n    14→ * This endpoint handles:\n    15→ * 1. POST with { type: \"link\", guildId, listingId } - Link a Discord server to a listing\n    16→ *    (called by the bot or by an admin setup flow)\n    17→ *\n    18→ * Note: Unlike Telegram's push-based webhook, Discord uses Gateway (WebSocket)\n    19→ * for real-time events. This endpoint is used as an HTTP callback for\n    20→ * simpler integration scenarios or manual triggering.\n    21→ */\n    22→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n    23→  // Verify webhook secret — fail closed if not configured\n    24→  const webhookSecret = process.env.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET;\n    25→  if (!webhookSecret) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Webhook not configured\" }, { status: 500 });\n    27→  }\n    28→  const headerSecret = req.headers.get(\"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\");\n    29→  if (headerSecret !== webhookSecret) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  try {\n    34→    const body = await req.json();\n    35→    const { type } = body;\n    36→\n    37→    if (type === \"link\") {\n    38→      return handleLink(body);\n    39→    }\n    40→\n    41→    // Discord Interaction verification (for future slash command support)\n    42→    if (type === 1) {\n    43→      // PING - respond with PONG\n    44→      return NextResponse.json({ type: 1 });\n    45→    }\n    46→\n    47→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unknown event type\" }, { status: 400 });\n    48→  } catch (err) {\n    49→    console.error(\"Discord webhook error:\", err);\n    50→    return NextResponse.json(\n    51→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n    52→      { status: 500 }\n    53→    );\n    54→  }\n    55→}\n    56→\n    57→async function handleLink(body: {\n    58→  guildId: string;\n    59→  listingId: number;\n    60→  channelId?: string;\n    61→}) {\n    62→  const { guildId, listingId, channelId } = body;\n    63→\n    64→  if (!guildId || !listingId) {\n    65→    return NextResponse.json(\n    66→      { error: \"Missing guildId or listingId\" },\n    67→      { status: 400 }\n    68→    );\n    69→  }\n    70→\n    71→  if (typeof listingId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(listingId) || listingId < 1) {\n    72→    return NextResponse.json(\n    73→      { error: \"Invalid listingId\" },\n    74→      { status: 400 }\n    75→    );\n    76→  }\n    77→\n    78→  // Validate Discord snowflake IDs to prevent path traversal in API URLs\n    79→  if (typeof guildId !== \"string\" || !isValidSnowflake(guildId)) {\n    80→    return NextResponse.json(\n    81→      { error: \"Invalid guildId format\" },\n    82→      { status: 400 }\n    83→    );\n    84→  }\n    85→  if (channelId !== undefined && (typeof channelId !== \"string\" || !isValidSnowflake(channelId))) {\n    86→    return NextResponse.json(\n    87→      { error: \"Invalid channelId format\" },\n    88→      { status: 400 }\n    89→    );\n    90→  }\n    91→\n    92→  const listing = db\n    93→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    94→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    95→\n    96→  if (!listing) {\n    97→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    98→  }\n    99→\n   100→  if (!listing.is_full) {\n   101→    return NextResponse.json(\n   102→      { error: \"Cannot link Discord until the group is full\" },\n   103→      { status: 400 }\n   104→    );\n   105→  }\n   106→\n   107→  if (listing.discord_link) {\n   108→    return NextResponse.json(\n   109→      { error: \"Discord link already set for this listing\" },\n   110→      { status: 400 }\n   111→    );\n   112→  }\n   113→\n   114→  let targetChannelId = channelId;\n   115→\n   116→  // If no channel specified, create one\n   117→  if (!targetChannelId) {\n   118→    const bookTitle = String(listing.book_title || \"reading-group\");\n   119→    const channelName = `bookmate-${bookTitle\n   120→      .toLowerCase()\n   121→      .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, \"-\")\n   122→      .slice(0, 80)}`;\n   123→\n   124→    const channel = await createTextChannel(\n   125→      guildId,\n   126→      channelName,\n   127→      `Bookmate reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" by ${listing.book_author}`\n   128→    );\n   129→\n   130→    if (!channel) {\n   131→      return NextResponse.json(\n   132→        { error: \"Failed to create Discord channel. Make sure the bot has Manage Channels permission.\" },\n   133→        { status: 500 }\n   134→      );\n   135→    }\n   136→\n   137→    targetChannelId = channel.id;\n   138→  }\n   139→\n   140→  // Create invite link\n   141→  const inviteLink = await createChannelInvite(targetChannelId);\n   142→  if (!inviteLink) {\n   143→    return NextResponse.json(\n   144→      { error: \"Failed to create invite link. Make sure the bot has Create Instant Invite permission.\" },\n   145→      { status: 500 }\n   146→    );\n   147→  }\n   148→\n   149→  // Save to DB\n   150→  db.prepare(\n   151→    \"UPDATE listings SET discord_link = ?, discord_channel_id = ? WHERE id = ?\"\n   152→  ).run(inviteLink, targetChannelId, listingId);\n   153→\n   154→  // Notify all members\n   155→  const members = db\n   156→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   157→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   158→\n   159→  for (const member of members) {\n   160→    createNotification(\n   161→      member.user_id,\n   162→      listingId,\n   163→      \"discord_ready\",\n   164→      `The Discord channel for \"${listing.book_title}\" is ready! Join to start discussing.`\n   165→    );\n   166→  }\n   167→\n   168→  // Send welcome message to the channel\n   169→  await sendChannelMessage(\n   170→    targetChannelId,\n   171→    `Welcome to the **${listing.book_title}** reading group! 📚\\n\\n` +\n   172→      `This channel was created by Bookmate for your reading group.\\n` +\n   173→      `**Reading pace:** ${listing.reading_pace}\\n` +\n   174→      `**Start date:** ${listing.start_date}\\n\\n` +\n   175→      `Happy reading! 🎉`\n   176→  );\n   177→\n   178→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, inviteLink });\n   179→}\n   180→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"af8e20d7-729c-4922-9889-91614f9e372a","uuid":"252a9476-272d-4c54-9297-b85d0f62bce7","timestamp":"2026-03-24T05:04:02.903Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/discord/webhook/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport {\n  createTextChannel,\n  createChannelInvite,\n  sendChannelMessage,\n  isValidSnowflake,\n} from \"@/lib/discord\";\nimport { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n\n/**\n * Discord webhook endpoint for bot events.\n *\n * This endpoint handles:\n * 1. POST with { type: \"link\", guildId, listingId } - Link a Discord server to a listing\n *    (called by the bot or by an admin setup flow)\n *\n * Note: Unlike Telegram's push-based webhook, Discord uses Gateway (WebSocket)\n * for real-time events. This endpoint is used as an HTTP callback for\n * simpler integration scenarios or manual triggering.\n */\nexport async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n  // Verify webhook secret — fail closed if not configured\n  const webhookSecret = process.env.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET;\n  if (!webhookSecret) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Webhook not configured\" }, { status: 500 });\n  }\n  const headerSecret = req.headers.get(\"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\");\n  if (headerSecret !== webhookSecret) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const body = await req.json();\n    const { type } = body;\n\n    if (type === \"link\") {\n      return handleLink(body);\n    }\n\n    // Discord Interaction verification (for future slash command support)\n    if (type === 1) {\n      // PING - respond with PONG\n      return NextResponse.json({ type: 1 });\n    }\n\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unknown event type\" }, { status: 400 });\n  } catch (err) {\n    console.error(\"Discord webhook error:\", err);\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n      { status: 500 }\n    );\n  }\n}\n\nasync function handleLink(body: {\n  guildId: string;\n  listingId: number;\n  channelId?: string;\n}) {\n  const { guildId, listingId, channelId } = body;\n\n  if (!guildId || !listingId) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Missing guildId or listingId\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (typeof listingId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(listingId) || listingId < 1) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Invalid listingId\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Validate Discord snowflake IDs to prevent path traversal in API URLs\n  if (typeof guildId !== \"string\" || !isValidSnowflake(guildId)) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Invalid guildId format\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n  if (channelId !== undefined && (typeof channelId !== \"string\" || !isValidSnowflake(channelId))) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Invalid channelId format\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n  }\n\n  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Cannot link Discord until the group is full\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (listing.discord_link) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Discord link already set for this listing\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  let targetChannelId = channelId;\n\n  // If no channel specified, create one\n  if (!targetChannelId) {\n    const bookTitle = String(listing.book_title || \"reading-group\");\n    const channelName = `bookmate-${bookTitle\n      .toLowerCase()\n      .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, \"-\")\n      .slice(0, 80)}`;\n\n    const channel = await createTextChannel(\n      guildId,\n      channelName,\n      `Bookmate reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" by ${listing.book_author}`\n    );\n\n    if (!channel) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Failed to create Discord channel. Make sure the bot has Manage Channels permission.\" },\n        { status: 500 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    targetChannelId = channel.id;\n  }\n\n  // Create invite link\n  const inviteLink = await createChannelInvite(targetChannelId);\n  if (!inviteLink) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Failed to create invite link. Make sure the bot has Create Instant Invite permission.\" },\n      { status: 500 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Save to DB\n  db.prepare(\n    \"UPDATE listings SET discord_link = ?, discord_channel_id = ? WHERE id = ?\"\n  ).run(inviteLink, targetChannelId, listingId);\n\n  // Notify all members\n  const members = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n\n  for (const member of members) {\n    createNotification(\n      member.user_id,\n      listingId,\n      \"discord_ready\",\n      `The Discord channel for \"${listing.book_title}\" is ready! Join to start discussing.`\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Send welcome message to the channel\n  await sendChannelMessage(\n    targetChannelId,\n    `Welcome to the **${listing.book_title}** reading group! 📚\\n\\n` +\n      `This channel was created by Bookmate for your reading group.\\n` +\n      `**Reading pace:** ${listing.reading_pace}\\n` +\n      `**Start date:** ${listing.start_date}\\n\\n` +\n      `Happy reading! 🎉`\n  );\n\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, inviteLink });\n}\n","numLines":180,"startLine":1,"totalLines":180}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_017AR2MXGRKaq3GA8ignTs5A","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import { isBotConfigured, setWebhook, getBotInfo } from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n     3→\n     4→/**\n     5→ * POST /api/telegram/setup\n     6→ *\n     7→ * Sets up the Telegram webhook. Should be called once after deployment.\n     8→ * Requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL env vars.\n     9→ *\n    10→ * Protected by a setup secret to prevent unauthorized access.\n    11→ */\n    12→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n    13→  const setupSecret = process.env.TELEGRAM_SETUP_SECRET;\n    14→  if (!setupSecret) {\n    15→    return NextResponse.json(\n    16→      { error: \"TELEGRAM_SETUP_SECRET not configured\" },\n    17→      { status: 500 }\n    18→    );\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const body = await req.json();\n    22→  if (body.secret !== setupSecret) {\n    23→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  if (!isBotConfigured()) {\n    27→    return NextResponse.json(\n    28→      { error: \"TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN not configured\" },\n    29→      { status: 500 }\n    30→    );\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  const webhookUrl = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL;\n    34→  if (!webhookUrl) {\n    35→    return NextResponse.json(\n    36→      { error: \"TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL not configured\" },\n    37→      { status: 500 }\n    38→    );\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  const bot = await getBotInfo();\n    42→  if (!bot) {\n    43→    return NextResponse.json(\n    44→      { error: \"Failed to connect to Telegram API. Check your bot token.\" },\n    45→      { status: 500 }\n    46→    );\n    47→  }\n    48→\n    49→  const success = await setWebhook(webhookUrl);\n    50→  if (!success) {\n    51→    return NextResponse.json(\n    52→      { error: \"Failed to set webhook\" },\n    53→      { status: 500 }\n    54→    );\n    55→  }\n    56→\n    57→  return NextResponse.json({\n    58→    ok: true,\n    59→    bot: { username: bot.username, id: bot.id },\n    60→    webhookUrl,\n    61→  });\n    62→}\n    63→\n    64→/**\n    65→ * GET /api/telegram/setup\n    66→ *\n    67→ * Returns the current Telegram bot configuration status.\n    68→ */\n    69→export async function GET() {\n    70→  return NextResponse.json({\n    71→    botConfigured: isBotConfigured(),\n    72→    webhookUrlConfigured: !!process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL,\n    73→  });\n    74→}\n    75→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"af8e20d7-729c-4922-9889-91614f9e372a","uuid":"051e986b-50cf-4947-8e2e-7d0d358a3f73","timestamp":"2026-03-24T05:04:02.906Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/telegram/setup/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport { isBotConfigured, setWebhook, getBotInfo } from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n\n/**\n * POST /api/telegram/setup\n *\n * Sets up the Telegram webhook. Should be called once after deployment.\n * Requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL env vars.\n *\n * Protected by a setup secret to prevent unauthorized access.\n */\nexport async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n  const setupSecret = process.env.TELEGRAM_SETUP_SECRET;\n  if (!setupSecret) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"TELEGRAM_SETUP_SECRET not configured\" },\n      { status: 500 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  const body = await req.json();\n  if (body.secret !== setupSecret) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n  }\n\n  if (!isBotConfigured()) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN not configured\" },\n      { status: 500 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  const webhookUrl = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL;\n  if (!webhookUrl) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL not configured\" },\n      { status: 500 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  const bot = await getBotInfo();\n  if (!bot) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Failed to connect to Telegram API. Check your bot token.\" },\n      { status: 500 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  const success = await setWebhook(webhookUrl);\n  if (!success) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Failed to set webhook\" },\n      { status: 500 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    ok: true,\n    bot: { username: bot.username, id: bot.id },\n    webhookUrl,\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * GET /api/telegram/setup\n *\n * Returns the current Telegram bot configuration status.\n */\nexport async function GET() {\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    botConfigured: isBotConfigured(),\n    webhookUrlConfigured: !!process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL,\n  });\n}\n","numLines":75,"startLine":1,"totalLines":75}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Gf7wSNrbkn5UchoEd6fMev","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { checkRateLimit } from \"@/lib/rate-limit\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n     7→  const session = await requireAuth();\n     8→  if (!session) {\n     9→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    10→  }\n    11→\n    12→  // Rate limit: 10 listings per 15 minutes per user\n    13→  const rateLimitKey = `create-listing:${session.userId}`;\n    14→  const { allowed, retryAfter } = checkRateLimit(rateLimitKey, 10);\n    15→  if (!allowed) {\n    16→    return NextResponse.json(\n    17→      { error: \"Too many listings created. Please try again later.\" },\n    18→      { status: 429, headers: { \"Retry-After\": String(retryAfter) } }\n    19→    );\n    20→  }\n    21→\n    22→  try {\n    23→    const body = await req.json();\n    24→    const {\n    25→      bookTitle,\n    26→      bookAuthor,\n    27→      bookCoverUrl,\n    28→      bookOlid,\n    29→      language,\n    30→      readingPace,\n    31→      startDate,\n    32→      meetingFormat,\n    33→      maxGroupSize,\n    34→      requiresApproval,\n    35→      platformPreference,\n    36→    } = body;\n    37→\n    38→    if (!bookTitle || !bookAuthor || !readingPace || !startDate || !meetingFormat || !maxGroupSize) {\n    39→      return NextResponse.json(\n    40→        { error: \"Missing required fields\" },\n    41→        { status: 400 }\n    42→      );\n    43→    }\n    44→\n    45→    // Input length limits to prevent abuse\n    46→    if (bookTitle.length > 300 || bookAuthor.length > 200 || readingPace.length > 200) {\n    47→      return NextResponse.json(\n    48→        { error: \"One or more fields exceed maximum length\" },\n    49→        { status: 400 }\n    50→      );\n    51→    }\n    52→\n    53→    if (![\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    54→      return NextResponse.json(\n    55→        { error: \"Invalid meeting format\" },\n    56→        { status: 400 }\n    57→      );\n    58→    }\n    59→\n    60→    if (!startDate || !/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDate)) {\n    61→      return NextResponse.json(\n    62→        { error: \"Invalid start date format (expected YYYY-MM-DD)\" },\n    63→        { status: 400 }\n    64→      );\n    65→    }\n    66→\n    67→    const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n    68→    if (startDate < today) {\n    69→      return NextResponse.json(\n    70→        { error: \"Start date cannot be in the past\" },\n    71→        { status: 400 }\n    72→      );\n    73→    }\n    74→\n    75→    const size = parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10);\n    76→    if (isNaN(size) || size < 2 || size > 20) {\n    77→      return NextResponse.json(\n    78→        { error: \"Group size must be between 2 and 20\" },\n    79→        { status: 400 }\n    80→      );\n    81→    }\n    82→\n    83→    const platform = [\"telegram\", \"discord\"].includes(platformPreference)\n    84→      ? platformPreference\n    85→      : \"telegram\";\n    86→\n    87→    // Validate book cover URL — only allow Open Library covers to prevent SSRF/tracking\n    88→    const sanitizedCoverUrl =\n    89→      bookCoverUrl && typeof bookCoverUrl === \"string\" &&\n    90→      bookCoverUrl.startsWith(\"https://covers.openlibrary.org/\")\n    91→        ? bookCoverUrl\n    92→        : \"\";\n    93→\n    94→    // Wrap listing creation + auto-join in a transaction for atomicity\n    95→    const createTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    96→      const result = db\n    97→        .prepare(\n    98→          `INSERT INTO listings (author_id, book_title, book_author, book_cover_url, book_olid, language, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size, requires_approval, platform_preference)\n    99→           VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`\n   100→        )\n   101→        .run(\n   102→          session.userId,\n   103→          bookTitle.trim(),\n   104→          bookAuthor.trim(),\n   105→          sanitizedCoverUrl,\n   106→          bookOlid || \"\",\n   107→          language || \"English\",\n   108→          readingPace.trim(),\n   109→          startDate,\n   110→          meetingFormat,\n   111→          size,\n   112→          requiresApproval ? 1 : 0,\n   113→          platform\n   114→        );\n   115→\n   116→      // Auto-join the author as a member\n   117→      db.prepare(\n   118→        \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n   119→      ).run(result.lastInsertRowid, session.userId);\n   120→\n   121→      return result.lastInsertRowid;\n   122→    });\n   123→\n   124→    const listingId = createTransaction();\n   125→\n   126→    return NextResponse.json(\n   127→      { id: listingId },\n   128→      { status: 201 }\n   129→    );\n   130→  } catch (error) {\n   131→    console.error(\"Create listing error:\", error);\n   132→    return NextResponse.json(\n   133→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n   134→      { status: 500 }\n   135→    );\n   136→  }\n   137→}\n   138→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"af8e20d7-729c-4922-9889-91614f9e372a","uuid":"fac4533f-c1ef-461d-bc3f-2339699eeff0","timestamp":"2026-03-24T05:04:03.398Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/create/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\nimport { checkRateLimit } from \"@/lib/rate-limit\";\n\nexport async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  // Rate limit: 10 listings per 15 minutes per user\n  const rateLimitKey = `create-listing:${session.userId}`;\n  const { allowed, retryAfter } = checkRateLimit(rateLimitKey, 10);\n  if (!allowed) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Too many listings created. Please try again later.\" },\n      { status: 429, headers: { \"Retry-After\": String(retryAfter) } }\n    );\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const body = await req.json();\n    const {\n      bookTitle,\n      bookAuthor,\n      bookCoverUrl,\n      bookOlid,\n      language,\n      readingPace,\n      startDate,\n      meetingFormat,\n      maxGroupSize,\n      requiresApproval,\n      platformPreference,\n    } = body;\n\n    if (!bookTitle || !bookAuthor || !readingPace || !startDate || !meetingFormat || !maxGroupSize) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Missing required fields\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    // Input length limits to prevent abuse\n    if (bookTitle.length > 300 || bookAuthor.length > 200 || readingPace.length > 200) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"One or more fields exceed maximum length\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (![\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Invalid meeting format\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (!startDate || !/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDate)) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Invalid start date format (expected YYYY-MM-DD)\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n    if (startDate < today) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Start date cannot be in the past\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    const size = parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10);\n    if (isNaN(size) || size < 2 || size > 20) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Group size must be between 2 and 20\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    const platform = [\"telegram\", \"discord\"].includes(platformPreference)\n      ? platformPreference\n      : \"telegram\";\n\n    // Validate book cover URL — only allow Open Library covers to prevent SSRF/tracking\n    const sanitizedCoverUrl =\n      bookCoverUrl && typeof bookCoverUrl === \"string\" &&\n      bookCoverUrl.startsWith(\"https://covers.openlibrary.org/\")\n        ? bookCoverUrl\n        : \"\";\n\n    // Wrap listing creation + auto-join in a transaction for atomicity\n    const createTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n      const result = db\n        .prepare(\n          `INSERT INTO listings (author_id, book_title, book_author, book_cover_url, book_olid, language, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size, requires_approval, platform_preference)\n           VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`\n        )\n        .run(\n          session.userId,\n          bookTitle.trim(),\n          bookAuthor.trim(),\n          sanitizedCoverUrl,\n          bookOlid || \"\",\n          language || \"English\",\n          readingPace.trim(),\n          startDate,\n          meetingFormat,\n          size,\n          requiresApproval ? 1 : 0,\n          platform\n        );\n\n      // Auto-join the author as a member\n      db.prepare(\n        \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n      ).run(result.lastInsertRowid, session.userId);\n\n      return result.lastInsertRowid;\n    });\n\n    const listingId = createTransaction();\n\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { id: listingId },\n      { status: 201 }\n    );\n  } catch (error) {\n    console.error(\"Create listing error:\", error);\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n      { status: 500 }\n    );\n  }\n}\n","numLines":138,"startLine":1,"totalLines":138}}}
